title: The public’s views on climate policies in seven large global south countries
authors: Richard T. Carson; Jiajun Lu; Emily A. Khossravi; Gunnar Köhlin; Erik Sterner; Thomas Sterner; Dale Whittington

tax_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: carbon tax revenue / revenues from carbon taxes
tax_mentions.context: Best–worst ranking of how to use revenues from a carbon tax (health/education, subsidized solar, clean R&D, roads, deficit reduction, rebates).
tax_mentions.page_or_section: Table 6; “Attitudes towards climate policies” (carbon tax revenue uses task)
tax_mentions.notes: Highest: education/health, subsidized solar, clean R&D; lowest: equal shares and deficit reduction.

inclusion_reason: Survey respondents in seven countries rank acceptability of carbon tax revenue earmarking (Table 6), including health/education, solar/R&D support, rebates, and deficit reduction.
